reforms.description: The Canada Foundation for Innovation was established with an investment of C$ 800 million over five years to modernize and enhance research infrastructure at post-secondary institutions and hospitals, thereby supporting research and innovation capacity in Canada.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing research infrastructure is expected to improve research capacity and innovation outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The Canada Foundation for Innovation has been operational since its establishment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a national foundation for innovation represents a significant structural change in research funding.
reforms.source_quote: The creation of a Canada Foundation for Innovation to modernise and enhance research infrastructure at post-secondary institutions and hospitals.

reforms.description: Canada introduced a refundable R&D tax credit for small firms, broadening the definition of R&D and technology for tax purposes, which aims to stimulate private sector investment in research and development.
reforms.package_name: R&D Tax Credit for Small Firms
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Stimulating private sector investment in R&D is expected to enhance innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit has been in effect since its introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a refundable tax credit represents a significant reform in the innovation support system.
reforms.source_quote: Introduced refundable R&D tax credit for small firms; broadened the definition of R&D and technology for tax purposes.

reforms.description: The Canadian government has improved the tax treatment of human capital investment through enhanced deductions for tuition and increased contribution limits to Registered Education Savings Plans, while also allocating C$ 500 million for a ten-year research and development fund in Ontario and stabilizing funding for the Industrial Research Assistance Program (IRAP) at C$ 97 million per year to encourage research and innovation by small and medium-sized enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in human capital and R&D funding is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce and stimulate innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: These reforms have been operational since their announcement.
reforms.importance_rationale: While these reforms are significant, they do not represent a structural overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The tax treatment of human capital investment has been improved. Deductions for tuition and for students attending educational institutions have been enhanced.
